title: I made a profit of 200,000 in the stock market over the past year, and my overall return outperformed the market.
author: Dramatic_Jackfruit57
selftext:
I’ve been quite fortunate over the past year, my total returns actually outperformed the broader market! While a favorable market environment meant I didn't truly face a rigorous test of my skills, I feel that many of the good habits I’ve cultivated are ones I can sustain over the long term.

Given that the U.S. stock market is characterized by long bull runs and short bear markets, my strategy,as a long-term investor,is to identify high-quality companies and hold onto their shares firmly. Whenever there is a dip in price, I simply continue to buy more.

My definition of a high-quality company is one with strong fundamentals, a competitive advantage, and a reasonable price range,not too inflated. For long-term investment, I don't aim to buy at the lowest point and sell at the highest, that's too energy-consuming, and in the long run, the difference might not be that significant.

I insist on holding good companies at reasonable prices, avoiding chasing highs, short selling, panic selling, and buying short-term options.

I steer clear of options and leverage entirely. Options are very close to gambling because short-term changes in stock value are not proportional to actual value. Many factors can cause a company to perform well but its stock price to suddenly drop. It is also difficult for retail investors like us to know this information, so I personally choose to only hold stocks.

These are some insights from a stock investment enthusiast like myself. I welcome any thoughts and suggestions from everyone, and I encourage a rational discussion. Please refrain from criticism and feel free to share your opinions in a friendly manner!
